title: 30 Min Meeting between Victor Coisne
meeting_title: 30 Min Meeting between Victor Coisne and Mara Leighton

so for me, the only way I'm going to keep recommending growthx for DevTool companies is if I can say with confidence that we're pushing the bar in terms of quality and in terms of customization.
Because those are the things that matter to DevTool people.
Yeah, and I certainly think of you guys.
I says, like, some of our, or at least within the clients that I work with, like, some of our best collaborators.
So to your point, like, I do think this is something that is repeatable.

And then also, yeah, I think that's actually the main thing.
So anyway, I think that I completely agree.
I understand.

understand, like, you can't give us more engineering time.

So, I really think there is an opportunity for you guys in that space.

How do I put this?

think people, there's fewer traffic, but traffic is of better quality.

So that's why I think the Kappa work makes a lot of sense.
It's like making sure like we identify the content gaps.
But we're not going to see traffic on those pages, if that makes sense.
It's going to be really hard to measure the impact of those pieces of content.

And that's really what, you know, because having like a bunch of articles that all bring like 200 to 300 like views, that's nice and all.

That means like it's okay.
It's a really good piece of content.
It's really timely.
And to me, that's really where I can say this is this really speaks to the quality like any kind of one viral.
was so it means like everything was worked well from the timing, from the quality, from the depths, from like a distribution standpoint, it really speaks to the full content lifecycle.
So, yeah, that's that's where my head's at.
Does that make sense?
It does make sense.

Is that still the way to think about it?

Yeah, signups for sure.

So getting to like maybe like 10 pieces a week and that would allow us more shots on goal, but I don't know if that, if there is appetite for that, but that's just one option to increase volume.

I mean, it's not.

I'll say.
Because I'm not just looking to externalize everything.
I want my team to be in the loop because it is critical for us.

we need to level up in terms of like, you know, that content is very core to what we do.
And I think we have a lot of overlap in terms of product.

And so, like, it's not as heavy, like, human heavy.

Is there anything that you have in mind in particular when you're saying like, we want a little bit more autonomy, maybe like you can pass some things on to us?

Is there anything in particular that you're thinking of that like maybe gives you a little bit of pause about not still owning or that you feel like would be relatively easy for the strapi team to own?

like you just plot your content in first, like, so those things will be internalized.

Yeah.
So, and same thing with content refresh.
I think like that's something that we, we can definitely do more there.
um, yeah, I think we'd, yeah, that makes sense.
Things where.
Okay, great.
That makes perfect sense.

And I think with the, that's helpful just to help me picture, like, so that something actually comes out of the conversation and you feel like your feedback's actually being applied, um, with the content refreshes.

That makes

Workflow.
So what's the company's stance on giving access to folks to self-serve their own workflows?
Yeah.
I don't have an updated timeline for you or the official company messaging on that.

What I'm able to share, I don't have a timeline, but to the same point as we discussed earlier, if we're rolling it out to certain partners to test and get their feedback, I'll make sure that the Strapi team is included in that, just knowing you've expressed the interest, you're great partners to us, we would value your product feedback.
So TLDR, I don't have an updated timeline for you right now, I'll see if I can get one, and then I'll just, of course, advocate for Strapi being involved in any kind of like pilot that we might do.
Cool.
That sounds great.

And, you know, vice versa, if you want to make sure you guys are really happy with your Strapi website, so if there's anything I can do on that end, I'd be happy to return the favor, give you guys access to You know, I don't know that you, I think you're a cloud customer, but I don't know that you're like a feature customer, so like if we want to give you like a free growth plan, like so you can get
There's a bunch of things that come with that.
So internally, I don't know, and I'd love to know what's the use case, how much content are you, how much are you investing on your own website?
I'd love to know about that so we can be a better partner and, you know, can make it a win-win.
Great, I appreciate that.
I'll pass that along to the team.
I don't have, I'm actually not totally sure, which makes sense, but I'll pass that along and see if there's anything.
And then just based on this conversation, I'll probably pass along to the team that we're going to do flat renewal, which makes sense, given everything that we've discussed.
I'll also just speak to engineering and see if there is any capacity to just take the CAP integration a little bit further.
And also keeping Strappy top of mind for Atlas self-serve.

If other customers within GrowthX are piloting something that might be useful to Strapi and I hear of it, I'd be happy to see if we can apply it to the work that we're doing.
So yeah, I think that's a general gist.
Cool.
Yeah, I think you summarized it well.
Good.
Okay, well, thanks for the time.
I really appreciate it.
Enjoy your weekend and enjoy the soccer game.
Yeah, thank you.
appreciate it.
Talk to you soon.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss Strapi's renewal with GrowthX, focusing on customization requests, content strategy, and partnership evolution.](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=15.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Strapi seeks CAPA integration as a priority; GrowthX to explore feasibility with engineering](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
  - [Focus on top-of-funnel (TOFU) content with high-impact pieces (1000+ views) as success metric](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [Strapi aims for more autonomy in content workflow, including metadata and refreshes](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)
  - [Flat renewal likely; GrowthX to prioritize repeatable, high-value customizations](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=1046.0)

## Topics

### CAPA Integration and Custom Engineering

  - [Strapi team frustrated by perceived delay in CAPA integration](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=15.0)
  - [Mara to prioritize discussion with GrowthX engineering within a week](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=66.0)
  - [Victor emphasizes value of custom flow control for DevTool companies](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=31.0)
  - [GrowthX to explore low-lift solutions, understanding flat renewal constraints](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=190.0)

### Content Strategy and Distribution

  - [80-20 split on TOFU vs. MOFU content agreed upon](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=461.0)
  - [Victor predicts shift in website traffic patterns; emphasis on TOFU content](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=461.0)
  - [Success metric: articles hitting 1000+ views, indicating quality and timeliness](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [Signups identified as key metric for 2026; focus on self-serve and onboarding](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=599.0)

### Partnership Evolution and Autonomy

  - [Strapi seeks deeper partnership with more autonomy in content workflow](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)
  - [GrowthX to potentially offload some tasks to Strapi team (e.g., metadata, content refreshes)](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)
  - [Strapi developing AI-powered metadata automation for images and SEO](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=822.0)
  - [Discussion on access to Atlas for self-serve workflows; timeline unclear](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)

### Mutual Benefits and Feedback

  - [Victor offers enhanced Strapi access (e.g., free growth plan) to GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [Interest in GrowthX's own website content strategy and Strapi usage](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [Both parties open to sharing product feedback and improvements](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Mara to discuss CAPA integration feasibility with GrowthX engineering team](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=66.0)
  - [Explore potential for Strapi's involvement in Atlas self-serve pilot program](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
  - [GrowthX to prioritize repeatable, high-value customizations for Strapi](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=1046.0)
  - [Mara to communicate flat renewal decision internally](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=1046.0)
  - [Victor to provide enhanced Strapi access options for GrowthX team](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [Continue focus on high-impact TOFU content creation and distribution](https://fathom.video/share/inczw1QNiskpm5wKz6AncQzHK1yfvPet?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)

action_items.description: Book eng review re: Kappa integration; then update Victor on feasibility/timeline
Ask GrowthX eng re: Atlas self-serve timeline; then advocate Strapi for pilot
Email GrowthX team: flat renewal for Strapi
